tech.title: Initial ARPANET four-node network completed
tech.description: The University of Utah comes online as the fourth node on ARPANET, completing the original four-node experimental computer network that will grow into today's internet.

tech.title: First PhD dissertation in computer science is presented
tech.description: J. Barkley Rosser presents what is widely regarded as the first PhD dissertation in computer science, helping establish computing as its own academic discipline.

history.title: Ghost ship Mary Celeste found abandoned in the Atlantic
history.description: The British brig Dei Gratia sights the American merchant ship Mary Celeste sailing erratically near the Azores. The ship is seaworthy and provisioned, but the crew has mysteriously vanished.

history.title: Prohibition ends in the United States
history.description: Utah becomes the 36th state to ratify the 21st Amendment, officially repealing the 18th Amendment and ending Prohibition after nearly 14 years of nationwide alcohol bans.

history.title: Flight 19 disappears over the Bermuda Triangle
history.description: Five U.S. Navy torpedo bombers on a training mission vanish over the Atlantic, along with a rescue plane sent to search for them, helping fuel legends of the Bermuda Triangle.

history.title: Great Smog of London begins
history.description: A thick, toxic smog caused by coal burning settles over London for five days, killing thousands and eventually leading to major clean air legislation in the United Kingdom.

history.title: Irish Free State–Northern Ireland border deal reached
history.description: Britain, the Irish Free State, and Northern Ireland sign an agreement in London that abandons plans to redraw the border and eases the Free State's financial obligations to Britain.

births.title: Martin Van Buren born
births.description: Future 8th president of the United States, Martin Van Buren, is born in Kinderhook, New York. He will serve one term in the White House from 1837 to 1841.

births.title: Walt Disney born
births.description: Animator and entertainment entrepreneur Walt Disney, co-founder of the Disney studio and creator of Mickey Mouse and Disneyland, is born in Chicago, Illinois.

births.title: Werner Heisenberg born
births.description: German theoretical physicist Werner Heisenberg, a pioneer of quantum mechanics best known for the uncertainty principle, is born in Würzburg, Germany.

deaths.title: Composer Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart dies
deaths.description: Prolific Austrian composer Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart, whose symphonies, operas, and concertos reshape Western music, dies in Vienna at age 35.

deaths.title: Nelson Mandela dies at 95
deaths.description: Nelson Mandela, anti-apartheid leader, political prisoner, and South Africa’s first Black president, dies in Johannesburg after decades as a global symbol of reconciliation.
